macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Exceed $137 Million with Bitcoin, Ethereum, and APE Leading Losses
items.summary: The crypto derivatives market saw over $137 million in liquidations in the past 24 hours, primarily affecting long positions in Bitcoin ($30+ million), Ethereum ($24.73 million), and APE ($81.96 million). This highlights ongoing market volatility and high leverage usage among traders.

items.title: Over 100 Crypto Firms Urge US Senate to Advance CLARITY Act for Regulatory Reform
items.summary: A coalition including Coinbase, Kraken, and Ripple sent a letter to the Senate Banking Committee demanding immediate passage of the Digital Asset Market CLARITY Act to replace agency lawsuits with clear policy. The push aims to stabilize US crypto business operations.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Holds Near $77,600 with Minor Decline; Ethereum Steady, Altcoins Mixed
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at around $77,638, down 0.2-0.3% in 24 hours, while Ethereum was up slightly near $2,300; XRP rose 0.5% to $1.44 and Solana gained 1% to $86.34 amid cooling rally post-rebound.

items.title: Ethereum NFT Trading Volume Surges to Nearly $4 Million, BAYC Up Over 266%
items.summary: Ethereum chain NFT volume reached $3.934 million in the past 24 hours, up 72.78%, with BAYC at $1.45 million (up 266%) and Countyard at $980,000 (up 88%). This indicates renewed interest in the NFT market.

items.title: President Trump Urges Congress to Pass Openness Act to Make USA Global Crypto Leader
items.summary: President Trump called on Congress to pass the Openness Act, positioning the US as the world's crypto leader. Separately, TRUMP is set to speak at a crypto conference, potentially boosting market sentiment.

items.title: BlackRock Clients Buy $167M Bitcoin; Firm Seeks Crypto Director Amid Institutional Surge
items.summary: BlackRock clients purchased $167.45 million in Bitcoin, and the firm is hiring a Bitcoin and crypto director. CEO Larry Fink highlighted Bitcoin's potential, signaling strong institutional interest.

items.title: Shiba Inu Burn Rate Surges 700% with 23.62 Million Tokens Burned in 24 Hours
items.summary: Shiba Inu saw a over 700% increase in burn rate, with 23.62 million SHIB tokens ($141) burned in the last 24 hours amid high trading activity. The 30-day total reached 232.81 million tokens worth $1,389.

items.title: Toncoin Reduces Fees to 0.00039 TON, Plans Feeless Transactions Soon
items.summary: Toncoin transaction fees dropped to 0.00039 TON, with upcoming feeless transactions unaffected by network load. BNB Chain leads Layer 1s in token holders.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta Plans 10% Workforce Cuts Amid Heavy AI Investments
items.summary: Meta is cutting approximately 10% of its workforce (around 8,000 jobs) and eliminating 6,000 open roles while investing tens of billions in AI, part of an industry-wide shift toward leaner operations with AI handling more tasks. Investors have reacted positively to similar moves by companies like Amazon, Microsoft, and Block.

items.title: Google Updates AI Studio, Announces TPU Infrastructure and Deep Research API
items.summary: Google's latest AI ecosystem updates include enhancements to AI Studio, new TPU infrastructure announcements, coding models, Antigravity, and the launch of Deep Research API. Discussion covers future directions post-coding models.

items.title: New Paper Proposes 'Learning Mechanics' as Emerging Scientific Theory of Deep Learning
items.summary: A paper by Jamie Simon, Daniel Kunin, and 12 co-authors argues that a scientific theory of deep learning is emerging, naming the field 'learning mechanics' to explain why deep learning performs so well.

items.title: Intel Upgraded to Outperform on Rising CPU Demand for AI Inference and Agents
items.summary: Analysts upgrade Intel citing surging CPU needs for AI workloads shifting to inference and agents, predicting a CPU-to-GPU ratio flip from 1:1 to 8:1, advanced packaging, and U.S. government push for domestic foundries.

items.title: EVO Open-Source AI Agent Project Gains 500 GitHub Stars in a Week
items.summary: EVO, an open-source AI agent, autonomously runs experiments, tests outcomes, discards failures, and improves software for any codebase, advancing beyond code-writing to full development cycles.

items.title: AI Startup Raises $30M at $500M Valuation for Total Funding of $47M
items.summary: An AI company announces a $30M financing round led by Craft Ventures at a $500M valuation, bringing total funding to $47M, with participation from Pace Capital, Chemistry, and TruArrow.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Microsoft Gaming Reverts to Xbox Branding
items.summary: Microsoft Gaming has announced a return to Xbox branding as part of a new mission statement. The change was discussed in a recent Game Mess Mornings stream covering multiple industry updates.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Sales Top Eight Million
items.summary: The game Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has achieved a major milestone with sales exceeding eight million units. This was highlighted in recent gaming news coverage.

items.title: Invincible VS Adds Story Mode from Animated Series Team
items.summary: Invincible VS is set to include a story mode developed by the team behind the Invincible animated series. The announcement was part of a broader gaming news roundup.

items.title: PS5 Firmware Update 13.20 Released
items.summary: Sony has released PS5 system software update 13.20, alongside updates for PS5HEN and Linux. Discussions also cover PSN game expiry policies for digital titles.

items.title: Fallout 76 PTS Patch Notes Incoming
items.summary: Fallout 76 is deploying an update to the public test server with changes to radiation and infestations. Patch notes are expected soon following the announcement.

items.title: Street Fighter 6 Next DLC Season Teased Amid Leak
items.summary: A leak has sparked discussions about the next DLC season for Street Fighter 6. Related fighting game news includes reactions to Fatal Fury updates.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Intel Stock Surges 23.6% in Best Day Since 1987 Amid Turnaround Signs
items.summary: Intel's shares closed up 23.6%, marking its best performance since October 1987, driven by renewed growth signals and CEO progress on operations and balance sheet strengthening. The stock has risen 124% year-to-date.

items.title: Samsung Mobile Division Faces First Annual Loss Due to RAM and Storage Shortages
items.summary: Samsung co-CEO TM Roh warned leaders that the MX mobile division could report its first-ever annual loss this year, amid skyrocketing costs from RAM shortages impacting smartphones.

items.title: Citizen Lab Uncovers Two Spying Campaigns Exploiting SS7 and Diameter Protocols
items.summary: Security researchers detailed two campaigns abusing weaknesses in SS7 and Diameter protocols across 2G to 5G networks to track locations, highlighting global telecom vulnerabilities.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon-Based Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion achieved a milestone with the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs to Challenge Microsoft and Apple
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs into the mainstream, scaling the mobile OS to desktops in a bid to reshape personal computing.
